q_definition: lane2 + lane7; lane3 is cascade-flux diagnostic only
cycle_warning_status: diagnostic only in a single trace; falsifying resonant-cycle status requires a forced/no-forcing comparison manifest
t_star_rule: first post-burn-in sample whose lane7 reaches the requested fraction of the observed lane7 drop; if no positive lane7 drop is observed, the first post-burn-in sample is used
falsification_rule: Preserve failures.  For a reproducible trace family whose shell-normalization guard fails, the report fails closed.  Otherwise this candidate encoding is falsified if post-burn-in Q_log has more than the allowed tolerated increases, if v2 shell movement contradicts encoded descent, or if a forced-run EV5 cycle survives the no-forcing/control comparison.  This single-trace adapter flags cycle warnings but does not promote them to resonant-cycle falsifiers without the comparison manifest.  This is evidence-only and does not prove or disprove Navier-Stokes regularity.

theta_profile.reason: no shell cutoffs at or above K_star in finite theta vector
theta_profile.definition: |dE_tail/dt + Diss_tail| / Diss_tail, computed for every cutoff shell k >= K_star and every transition time t
theta_profile.finite_vector_semantics: theta_profile.csv is the finite vector of theta(k,t) rows over the fixed cutoff set k>=K_star and observed transition times
theta_profile.danger_shell_rule: argmax over the fixed-cutoff per-shell sup profile theta_k = sup_t theta(k,t)
